---
title: "Traits and AquaX Sample"
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
    message: false
    warning: false
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(terra)
```

```{r}
# Not on server
load('data/META_02122025.Rdata')
```

## Build species information

Associate SDM files with the metadata for the 564 sampled species
```{r}
spp_fs <- list.files('/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/2026_OHARA_TST_MORPHO/SDM',
                     full.names = TRUE)

df <- data.frame(f = spp_fs) %>%
    mutate(fsize = file.size(f),
           aphiaid = str_extract(basename(f), '[0-9]+') %>% as.integer()) %>%
    left_join(META, by = c('aphiaid' = 'AphiaID'))
```

## Inspect a test species

Identify the structure of the SDM .Rdata for a single species

```{r}
# need to figure out this issue 
load('/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/2026_OHARA_TST_MORPHO/SDM/FINAL_EMSDM_EMMEAN_SP_550138.Rdata')
```



```{r}
set.seed(123)
test_spp <- slice_sample(df, n = 1)

load(test_spp$f)

summary(FINALEMMEAN)

r <- rast(FINALEMMEAN)
names(r)
plot(r$Current)
plot(r$Current_NR)
plot(r$RCP85_2100)
```

```{r}
# Assign historical, future, (x-y will be pixel id), and change between historical and future 
finalemmean_test <- FINALEMMEAN %>% 
  mutate(aphiaid = test_spp$aphiaid,
         xyid = paste(x, y, sep = "_")) %>% 
  select(x, y, xyid, aphiaid, Current, RCP85_2050, cutoff) %>% 
  mutate(historical = case_when(Current < cutoff ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1),
         future = case_when(RCP85_2050 < cutoff ~ 0,
                             TRUE ~ 1)) %>%
  # historical column plus 2 * future gives 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence
  mutate(pxl_change = historical + 2 * future)

```

```{r}
r_pa <- rast(finalemmean_test)
plot(r_pa$historical)
plot(r_pa$future)
plot(r_pa$pxl_change)

```
```{r}
# Invasion and extinction 
finalemmean_inv_ext <- finalemmean_test %>%
  mutate(inv_ext = case_when(historical == 0 & future == 0 ~ 0, #absence
                             historical == 1 & future == 1 ~ 1, #presence
                             historical == 0 & future == 1 ~ 2, #invasion
                             historical == 1 & future == 0 ~ 3,)) #extinction
```

# Test a bigger subsample

```{r}
set.seed(123)
test_spp_30 <- slice_sample(df, n = 50)
files_30 <- test_spp_30$f
aphiaid_30 <- test_spp_30$aphiaid
#load(test_spp_30$f)

```

```{r}
test_1 <- test_spp_30 %>% 
  filter(aphiaid == aphiaid_30[18]) 

load(test_1$f)
```


```{r}
extract_change <- function(id, df) {
  spp_filter <- df %>% 
    filter(aphiaid == id) 
  
  load(spp_filter$f)
  
  spp_change <- FINALEMMEAN %>% 
  mutate(aphiaid = id) %>% 
  select(x, y, aphiaid, Current, RCP85_2050, cutoff) %>% 
  mutate(historical = case_when(Current < cutoff ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1),
         future = case_when(RCP85_2050 < cutoff ~ 0,
                             TRUE ~ 1)) %>%
  # historical column plus 2 * future gives 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence
  mutate(pxl_change = historical + 2 * future)
  
  #r_spp_change <- rast(spp_change)
  
  # return just the pixel change field
  #return(r_spp_change[["pxl_change"]])
  return(spp_change)
}
```

```{r}
change_rast <- lapply(aphiaid_30, FUN = extract_change, df = test_spp_30)
```

```{r}
x <- change_rast[[8]]
y <- change_rast[[11]]
```

```{r}
plot(x$pxl_change)
plot(y$pxl_change)
```

```{r}
freq(x$pxl_change)
freq(x$pxl_change, value = 3)
```


## Next, extract the rasters into a dataframe in order to count 
Or stick to raster math (could mask everything but 3s and count those up (the number of species in each cell that remain the same); then count up historical = 1 (historical species) and then could up future = 1 (future species) --> use these to calculate SSI (2 * same / historical + future) 
- challenge - rasters aren't the same size, but cells overlap 
- creating dataframe might create same number of rows

```{r}
raster_x_df <- data.frame(values(x)) #%>% 
  #mutate(pixel_id = row_number())
```

```{r}
raster_y_df <- data.frame(values(y)) #%>% 
  #mutate(pixel_id = row_number())
```

```{r}
# The rasters are not the same extent (different cropping based on species), so I need to union them to find the largest area and then pad the individual rasters
change_collection <- sprc(change_rast)
max_extent <- ext(change_collection)
```

```{r}
align_list <- function(r) {
  extend(r, max_extent)
}
```

```{r}
change_rast_aligned <- lapply(change_rast, align_list)
```

```{r}
change_stack <- rast(change_rast_aligned)
```

```{r}
# Count the number of "3s" - shared species
# adding row name? 3 works, since it's unique, but otherwise I ned to filter these layers before the stack 
spp_same_count <- sum(change_stack == 3, na.rm = TRUE)
spp_ext <- sum(change_stack == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
spp_inv <- sum(change_stack == 2, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
spp_ssi <- (2*spp_same_count)/((2*spp_same_count) + spp_ext + spp_inv)
```

```{r}
#Species richness - historic
plot(spp_same_count + spp_ext)
```

```{r}
#Species richness - future
plot(spp_same_count + spp_inv)
```


```{r}
# Species SSI
plot(spp_ssi)
```

## Try running in parallel with app function 

```{r}
count_change <- function(x){
  sum(x == 3, na.rm = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
system.time({
  spp_same_count_pl <- app(change_stack, count_change, cores = 5)
  })

```

```{r}
plot(spp_same_count_pl)
```



```{r}
library(parallel)
```

#doesn't work with SpatRasters (so going to convert to rasters outside function)

```{r}
aphiaid_df <- df$aphiaid
```

```{r}
# Took about 6 minutes
system.time({
  out_list <- parallel::mclapply(aphiaid_df, FUN = extract_change, df = df, mc.cores = 10)
})
```

```{r}
system.time({
  change_rast <- lapply(aphiaid_30, FUN = extract_change, df = test_spp_30)
})
```


```{r}
# Function to rasterize extract_change return and return only pxl_change
spp_r <- function(spp, col){
  r_spp_change <- rast(spp)
  return(r_spp_change[[col]])
}
```

```{r}
# Get rasters of pxl_change (took 20 min)
out_list_r <- lapply(out_list, spp_r, col = "pxl_change")
```

```{r}
plot(out_list_r[[1]])
```

```{r}
# The rasters are not the same extent (different cropping based on species), so I need to union them to find the largest area and then pad the individual rasters
change_collection <- sprc(out_list_r)
max_extent <- ext(change_collection)
```

```{r}
align_list <- function(r) {
  extend(r, max_extent)
}
```

```{r}
change_rast_aligned <- lapply(out_list_r, align_list)
```

```{r}
change_stack <- rast(change_rast_aligned)
```

```{r}
# Count the number of "3s" - shared species
# adding row name? 3 works, since it's unique, but otherwise I need to filter these layers before the stack 
spp_same_count <- sum(change_stack == 3, na.rm = TRUE)
spp_ext <- sum(change_stack == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
spp_inv <- sum(change_stack == 2, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
spp_ssi <- (2*spp_same_count)/((2*spp_same_count) + spp_ext + spp_inv)
```

```{r}
writeRaster(spp_ext, 
            filename = "/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/outputs/spp_ext_031626.tif")
writeRaster(spp_inv, 
            filename = "/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/outputs/spp_inv_031626.tif")
```

```{r}
writeRaster(spp_ssi, 
            filename = "/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/outputs/spp_ssi_03.16.26.tif")
```

```{r}
plot(spp_ssi)
```

```{r}
spp_ssi_df <- as.data.frame(spp_ssi, xy = TRUE)
spp_h_df <- as.data.frame(spp_ext + spp_same_count, xy = TRUE)
spp_f_df <- as.data.frame(spp_inv + spp_same_count, xy = TRUE)
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = spp_h_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = sum)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(0,200), direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "AquaX Subsample: 564 species historic SR", 
       fill = "SR")
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = spp_f_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = sum)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(0,200), direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "AquaX Subsample: 564 species future species count", 
       fill = "count")
```


```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = spp_ssi_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = sum)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(0,1), direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "AquaX Subsample: 564 species SSI", 
       fill = "SSI")
```

## Test out parallel rasters

```{r}
set.seed(123)
test_spp_30 <- slice_sample(df, n = 10)
files_30 <- test_spp_30$f
aphiaid_30 <- test_spp_30$aphiaid
#load(test_spp_30$f)

```

```{r}
out_list_sample <- out_list[1:10]
```


```{r}
# Function to rasterize extract_change return and return only pxl_change
spp_r_pll <- function(spp, col){
  r_spp_change <- rast(spp)
  r_spp_single <- r_spp_change[[col]]
  return(wrap(r_spp_single))
}
```

```{r}
# Get rasters of pxl_change (rasters are wrapped)
out_list_r_pll <- parallel::mclapply(out_list_sample, spp_r_pll, col = "pxl_change", mc.cores = 5)
```

```{r}
# Unwrap rasters
final_rasters <- lapply(out_list_r_pll, unwrap)
```

```{r}
plot(final_rasters[[1]])
```

## Test out the function with raster back in and parallel 

```{r}
extract_change <- function(id, df, col) {
  spp_filter <- df %>% 
    filter(aphiaid == id) 
  
  load(spp_filter$f)
  
  spp_change <- FINALEMMEAN %>% 
  mutate(aphiaid = id) %>% 
  select(x, y, aphiaid, Current, RCP85_2050, cutoff) %>% 
  mutate(historical = case_when(Current < cutoff ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1),
         future = case_when(RCP85_2050 < cutoff ~ 0,
                             TRUE ~ 1)) %>%
  # historical column plus 2 * future gives 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence
  mutate(pxl_change = historical + 2 * future)
  
  r_spp_change <- rast(spp_change)
  
  # return just the pixel change field
  r_spp_single <- r_spp_change[[col]]
  return(wrap(r_spp_single))
}
```

```{r}
# Took 5 mins.
system.time({
  out_list_r_pll <- parallel::mclapply(aphiaid_df, FUN = extract_change, df = df,
                                       col = "pxl_change",
                                       mc.cores = 10)
})
```

```{r}
# Unwrap rasters (30 seconds)
system.time({
  final_rasters_pll <- lapply(out_list_r_pll, unwrap)
  })
```

```{r}
# The rasters are not the same extent (different cropping based on species), so I need to union them to find the largest area and then pad the individual rasters
# LAA - could we just use extent of globe for the full analysis and skip these steps? (these steps take a handful of minutes)
change_collection <- sprc(final_rasters_pll)
max_extent <- ext(change_collection)
```

```{r}
align_list <- function(r) {
  extend(r, max_extent)
}
```

```{r}
change_rast_aligned <- lapply(final_rasters_pll, align_list)
```

```{r}
change_stack <- rast(change_rast_aligned)
```

## Try running sum function in parellel with terra's app

## Try running in parallel with app function 

```{r}
count_change <- function(x, n){
  sum(x == n, na.rm = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
# Took 8 min
system.time({
  spp_same_count_pl <- app(change_stack, count_change, n = 3, cores = 10)
  })
```

```{r}
plot(spp_same_count_pl)
```

```{r}
# Took 8 min
system.time({
  spp_inv_pl <- app(change_stack, count_change, n = 2, cores = 10)
  })
```

```{r}
# Took 8 min
system.time({
  spp_ext_pl <- app(change_stack, count_change, n = 1, cores = 10)
  })
```

```{r}
spp_ssi_pl <- (2*spp_same_count_pl)/((2*spp_same_count_pl) + spp_ext_pl + spp_inv_pl)
```

```{r}
plot(spp_ssi_pl)
```
